demo.title: Audio Analysis Demo
demo.subtitle: Drop an audio file to analyze BPM, key, chords, and more

quick.title: Quick Master
quick.dropSubtitle: WAV, MP3, FLAC, or OGG. Your audio never leaves this browser.
quick.processingEta: Est. processing time: {seconds}s on your device

studio.title: Studio
studio.renderHint: Runs the full chain on the loaded audio.
studio.resetModuleHint: Restore every knob in this module to its default value.
studio.resetModuleUseWhen: You've wandered far from the defaults and want to start over for this stage.

tooltips.savePreset: Save all current chain settings to your browser's local storage so they can be restored later.
tooltips.loadPreset: Replace current chain settings with the most recently saved preset from this browser.
tooltips.exportPreset: Create a JSON snapshot of every chain setting. Use this to share or back up a chain.
tooltips.importPreset: Load chain settings from a previously exported JSON file.

pop.name: Pop
pop.tagline: Balanced tone and loudness for modern releases.
pop.description: Transparent tilt EQ, gentle ~2:1 glue compression, and a touch of air-band lift. Targets contemporary pop loudness without crushing transients.
pop.tip: Mainstream pop, rock, indie, or singer-songwriter material aimed at streaming services.

edm.name: EDM / Dance
edm.tagline: Tighter dynamics with a controlled final limiter.
edm.description: Faster compressor release (≤120 ms) for movement on the kick + bass groove, plus a confident true-peak limiter for club-ready loudness.
edm.tip: House, techno, trance, drum & bass — anything that needs sustained energy on club systems.

acoustic.name: Acoustic
acoustic.tagline: Gentler compression that preserves natural transients.
acoustic.description: Lower ratio (1.5:1), slower attack/release, and softer EQ moves. The compressor protects without flattening, leaving dynamic detail intact.
acoustic.tip: Acoustic guitar, jazz, classical, or any production where natural dynamics matter.

hiphop.name: Hip-Hop / R&B
hiphop.tagline: Solid low end with focused vocal presence.
hiphop.description: Pivot shifted to 850 Hz with a slight downward tilt to keep weight in the low end, plus a stronger compressor ratio for cohesion.
hiphop.tip: Hip-hop, trap, R&B, lo-fi — anything that lives off a powerful low-end.

aiMusic.name: AI-Generated
aiMusic.tagline: Denoise, air-band lift, and repair for AI artifacts.
aiMusic.description: Activates spectral repair to suppress the high-frequency mush of generative models, then opens up the air band around 15.5 kHz.
aiMusic.tip: Suno, Udio, MusicGen, or any AI-rendered material that needs cleanup and added sparkle.

speech.name: Speech / Podcast
speech.tagline: Clear voice normalization for spoken content.
speech.description: Strict denoise, capped fast attack (≤8 ms) for consistent voice level, and zero stereo decorrelation to keep the voice rock-solid in the center.
speech.tip: Podcasts, audiobooks, lectures, voiceovers — content where intelligibility is paramount.

descriptions.spotify: Spotify normalizes playback to about -14 LUFS integrated. Tracks louder than that are turned down at playback.
descriptions.youtube: YouTube targets roughly -14 LUFS integrated for music; louder masters get attenuated on playback.
descriptions.apple: Apple Music targets about -16 LUFS integrated with strict true-peak limiting at -1 dBTP.
descriptions.tiktok: TikTok normalizes feed audio around -16 LUFS to keep volume consistent across short videos.
descriptions.custom: Pick any LUFS target between -24 and -8. Useful for non-streaming delivery like CD, vinyl, or broadcast.

useWhen.spotify: Delivering to Spotify or any service that targets -14 LUFS playback.
useWhen.youtube: Uploading to YouTube Music or YouTube videos that should match streaming norms.
useWhen.apple: Delivering to Apple Music or any service that prefers the quieter Apple target.
useWhen.tiktok: Short-form video platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, or Shorts.
useWhen.custom: Internal references, mastered CDs, vinyl pre-masters, or broadcast specs that don't match streaming.

hints.tone: Shifts the overall spectrum from neutral toward warmer harmonic color. Higher values add low-mid weight and tame top-end harshness.
hints.width: Adjusts stereo side energy while keeping lows centered. Low values tighten the image; high values open it up without breaking mono compatibility.
hints.dynamics: Sets how hard compression flattens the signal. Lower values sound denser and louder; higher values keep transient movement and punch.
hints.strength: How assertively the selected module shapes the signal. Higher values push the effect harder.
hints.inputGainDb: Level fed into the mastering chain. Keep peaks below 0 dBFS so downstream stages have headroom to work.
hints.denoiseAmount: Spectral denoise applied before tone shaping. Higher values remove more hiss and rumble but can dull transients.
hints.declickAmount: Detects and repairs sharp clicks/pops. Higher values lower the trigger threshold and catch quieter artifacts at the cost of some risk to drum transients.
hints.dereverbAmount: Spectral dereverb that suppresses late reflections. Higher values attenuate room tone more aggressively but can thin out sustained material.
hints.tiltDb: Tilt EQ pivoted around 1 kHz. Positive values brighten the highs and thin the lows; negative values do the opposite.
hints.compressorThresholdDb: Level where the compressor starts reducing gain. Lower values catch more of the signal; higher values touch only peaks.
hints.compressorRatio: How much louder-than-threshold signal is reduced. 2:1 is gentle glue, 4:1 is mastering control, 8:1+ is heavy limiting.
hints.compressorAttackMs: How quickly the compressor clamps after the signal crosses threshold. Faster attacks tame transients; slower ones let them through.
hints.compressorReleaseMs: How quickly the compressor recovers after the signal drops back below threshold. Tracks tempo for musical breathing.
hints.deesserAmount: Tames sibilant peaks around 6 kHz. Higher values lower the threshold and raise the ratio so the de-esser engages on softer s/sh content.
hints.transientAttackDb: Boosts (positive) or softens (negative) the attack portion of transients. Independent of overall level — drives drum punch without raising loudness.
hints.multibandLowAmount: Compression amount for the low band (<220 Hz). Higher values clamp boomy lows; useful for bass-heavy mixes that overpower the rest.
hints.multibandMidAmount: Compression amount for the mid band (220 Hz–4.5 kHz). Controls body and vocal presence; small moves go a long way here.
hints.multibandHighAmount: Compression amount for the high band (>4.5 kHz). Tames harshness or cymbal flare without dulling overall brightness.
hints.tapeDriveDb: Drive into the tape model. Higher values push more harmonic generation and natural compression; reads as warmth and glue.
hints.tapeSaturation: How hard the tape stage saturates at a given drive. Adds even/odd harmonics and softens transients in a vintage way.
hints.exciterAmount: Generates controlled high-frequency harmonics for perceived brightness without raw EQ boost.
hints.airBandAmount: Gentle shelf above ~10 kHz that adds openness and shimmer to the very top of the spectrum.
hints.stereoWidth: Multiplier on stereo side content. 1.0 leaves it untouched, <1.0 narrows, >1.0 widens — bass stays mono-safe.
hints.monoMakerAmount: Sums low frequencies to mono below a fixed crossover. Higher values move more of the bass into the center for club/vinyl safety.
hints.limiterCeilingDb: Maximum true-peak level allowed at the output. Lower ceilings give lossy-codec headroom; higher ones push more loudness.
hints.limiterLookaheadMs: How far ahead the limiter peeks before peaks arrive. More lookahead catches transients cleaner but adds latency.

tips.tone: Mixes feel thin, harsh, or fatiguing at the top.
tips.width: Tracks feel narrow on speakers but need to stay mono-safe.
tips.dynamics: Drums need punch (high) or streaming needs density (low).
tips.strength: Dial up if the effect is too subtle; pull back if it overcooks.
tips.inputGainDb: Source already clips, or sits too quietly for the chain to react.
tips.denoiseAmount: AI-generated, mobile-recorded, or restoration material with audible noise.
tips.declickAmount: Vinyl rips, digital glitches, or recordings with audible clicks/pops you want gone.
tips.dereverbAmount: Room-y or reverb-heavy sources where you need less ambience before mastering.
tips.tiltDb: Small moves of ±1–2 dB go a long way — tilt affects the whole spectrum.
tips.compressorThresholdDb: Aim for 3–6 dB of gain reduction on the loudest sections.
tips.compressorRatio: Stick to 1.5:1–3:1 for transparent mastering glue.
tips.compressorAttackMs: Slow (20–40 ms) preserves drum punch; fast (1–5 ms) tightens loose peaks.
tips.compressorReleaseMs: Match roughly one beat for musical pumping; faster on percussive material.
tips.deesserAmount: Vocals or cymbals where sibilance/harshness pokes out over the mix.
tips.transientAttackDb: Drums sound soft — push +1 to +3 dB to add bite without raising volume.
tips.multibandLowAmount: Sub or kick energy overwhelms the mix on club systems.
tips.multibandMidAmount: Mid range feels honky or vocals jump forward inconsistently.
tips.multibandHighAmount: Cymbal hits or sibilance flare with the rest of the mix staying calm.
tips.tapeDriveDb: Sterile or AI-generated material that needs analog warmth and glue.
tips.tapeSaturation: Push past 0.3 only when you want obvious tape character on the bus.
tips.exciterAmount: Subtle 0.1–0.3 adds sparkle without sounding buzzy or digital.
tips.airBandAmount: Vocals, acoustic, and ambient material that needs extra sheen on top.
tips.stereoWidth: Stay under ~1.3× to keep streaming codecs and club systems happy.
tips.monoMakerAmount: Vinyl pressing, club playback, or any environment where mono bass matters.
tips.limiterCeilingDb: -1.0 dBTP for Spotify/Apple; -0.3 dBTP for CD or uncompressed delivery.
tips.limiterLookaheadMs: 5 ms is the standard mastering setting; lower only for low-latency monitoring.

defaults.label: Default
defaults.tone: Center keeps tilt neutral; the EQ stage stays out of the way until you ask for warmth or air.
defaults.width: Center leaves the stereo image untouched so the imager only multiplies what you set in studio mode.
defaults.dynamics: Center balances compressor threshold/ratio between transparent glue and visible movement.
defaults.inputGainDb: Source enters the chain unchanged — loudness normalization handles the final level.
defaults.denoiseAmount: Bypassed because spectral denoise is destructive — enable only when the source actually has noise.
defaults.declickAmount: Bypassed; the click detector skips clean material to avoid chewing drum transients.
defaults.dereverbAmount: Bypassed; aggressive dereverb thins sustained material, so it's opt-in.
defaults.tiltDb: Flat tilt — leave the source's tonal balance alone unless mixes feel dull or harsh.
defaults.compressorThresholdDb: Typical mastering glue point — produces ~3 dB of gain reduction on the loudest sections.
defaults.compressorRatio: Light glue ratio that controls peaks without crushing transient detail.
defaults.compressorAttackMs: Fast enough to catch peaks, slow enough to preserve drum attack.
defaults.compressorReleaseMs: Musical breathing time that tracks roughly one beat at common pop tempos.
defaults.deesserAmount: Bypassed; engage only when sibilance actively pokes out over the mix.
defaults.transientAttackDb: No transient shaping — drum punch is preserved as recorded.
defaults.multibandLowAmount: Bypassed; reach for low-band control only when bass overwhelms the mix.
defaults.multibandMidAmount: Bypassed; tame mids only when honky midrange or jumpy vocals are an issue.
defaults.multibandHighAmount: Bypassed; tame highs only when cymbals or sibilance flare.
defaults.tapeDriveDb: No tape drive — clean source flavor; enable for analog glue/warmth.
defaults.tapeSaturation: Tape stage bypassed by default; pair with drive for analog character.
defaults.exciterAmount: Subtle harmonic seasoning — adds perceived brightness without obvious processing.
defaults.airBandAmount: Gentle ~10 kHz shelf for openness and shimmer on most material.
defaults.stereoWidth: 5% widening adds air without breaking mono compatibility on speakers and phones.
defaults.monoMakerAmount: Bypassed; turn on for vinyl pressing or club playback where mono bass matters.
defaults.limiterCeilingDb: Safe ceiling for Spotify/Apple — leaves codec headroom for lossy playback.
defaults.limiterLookaheadMs: Standard mastering lookahead — balanced between transient capture and latency.

intro.title: What is mastering?
intro.body: Mastering is the final step before release. It prepares a mix so it plays back reliably across streaming platforms, speakers, headphones, and phones.
intro.pointLoudness: Balances perceived loudness for the selected destination.
intro.pointTone: Adjusts broad tonal balance and air without rebuilding the mix.
intro.pointPeaks: Controls peaks so conversion and streaming playback stay clean.
intro.pointPrivacy: In this demo, audio processing runs locally in your browser.

input.name: Input Gain
input.description: Sets a clean starting level before repair, EQ, and dynamics.
input.tip: Trim the source to leave ~6 dB of headroom before the chain starts working.

repair.name: Repair
repair.description: Denoise, declick, and dereverb stages for cleaning up noisy, glitchy, or room-y sources before mastering.
repair.tip: Run only what you actually need — each stage has cost; leave everything at 0 for clean studio mixes.

eq.name: EQ
eq.description: Shapes tonal balance with a broad tilt before dynamics.
eq.tip: Use gentle tilt (±2 dB) to nudge overall brightness — not for surgical correction.

dynamics.name: Dynamics
dynamics.description: Single-band compressor plus de-esser and transient shaper for level control and detail.
dynamics.tip: Set compressor first for glue, then layer de-esser and transient shaping for polish.

multiband.name: Multiband Compressor
multiband.description: Splits the signal into low, mid, and high bands and compresses each independently.
multiband.tip: Reach for bands individually when one frequency range needs control without affecting the rest.

exciter.name: Saturation / Air
exciter.description: Tape, exciter, and air-band stages that add harmonic color and top-end openness.
exciter.tip: Tape glues the mix, exciter sharpens detail, air widens the top — combine in small amounts.

stereo.name: Stereo
stereo.description: Stereo imager and mono-maker — controls width and the bass-mono crossover.
stereo.tip: Open up dense mixes; narrow when the bass starts wandering off-center.

limiter.name: True Peak Limiter
limiter.description: Controls the final ceiling and catches inter-sample peaks.
limiter.tip: Always engaged on the final stage — adjust the ceiling rather than the input gain.

loudness.name: LUFS Normalize
loudness.description: Matches the rendered master to the selected platform loudness.
loudness.tip: Pick the destination platform and the chain compensates automatically.

output.name: Output
output.description: Prepares the final WAV download from the processed buffer.
output.tip: Verify the naming convention and bit depth before exporting your delivery file.
output.note: The browser demo exports stereo 16-bit PCM WAV and a JSON report.

descriptions.lufs: Integrated loudness (BS.1770). The perceived volume of the whole master — what streaming services normalize against.
descriptions.peak: Peak sample level in dBFS. The highest absolute sample value, before true-peak oversampling.
descriptions.crest: Peak-to-RMS ratio in dB. Higher = more dynamic; lower = more compressed/loud.
descriptions.correlation: L/R correlation between -1 (out of phase) and +1 (mono). Near 0 = wide stereo, near 1 = mono.
descriptions.phase: Lissajous/phase scope. Diagonal cluster = mono-safe, horizontal spread = wide, X shape = potential phase issues.

useWhen.lufs: Comparing the rendered loudness to your platform target — click to jump to the LUFS stage.
useWhen.peak: Checking digital ceiling headroom — click to jump to the limiter stage.
useWhen.crest: Diagnosing 'too squashed' or 'too dynamic' results — click to jump to the dynamics stage.
useWhen.correlation: Diagnosing stereo width problems — click to jump to the stereo stage.
useWhen.phase: Verifying mono compatibility before delivering to club/vinyl/broadcast.

result.title: Result
result.downloadHint: Save the rendered master as a stereo 16-bit PCM WAV file.
result.reportHint: Download a JSON metadata report including LUFS, peak, applied gain, and chain stages.
result.beforeHint: Listen to the unprocessed source for A/B comparison.
result.afterHint: Listen to the rendered master for A/B comparison.
result.loudnessMatchTooltip: Attenuates whichever side is louder so you compare tonal/dynamic changes, not just volume — required for fair A/B.
result.loudnessMatchNote: A/B playback lowers the louder side so the comparison is not biased by volume.
result.rawCompareNote: Raw comparison is using each file at its rendered level.

reference.title: Reference track
reference.titleHint: Load a reference track and the chain will run a spectral match instead of the normal chain. Output EQ is shaped toward the reference's spectrum.
reference.drop: Drop a reference track for comparison
reference.matchHint: Replaces the regular chain with a one-shot spectral match against the loaded reference. Only EQ shape is matched — loudness and limiting are not.

errors.loadFailed: Could not load this audio file. Try WAV, MP3, FLAC, or OGG.
errors.renderFailed: Mastering failed. Try a shorter file or a lighter preset.
errors.referenceFailed: Reference matching failed. Try a shorter source or reference file.
errors.noSavedPreset: No saved chain preset was found in this browser.
errors.presetLoadFailed: Could not load these chain settings.
